contracts.note: NVDA call buyers are aggressively lifting offers on expiring contracts, with today's volume already exceeding open interest and an additional 24k contracts traded this hour alone. The 5% out-of-the-money strike with zero days to expiration, trading on the ask, suggests a tactical bet on a significant intraday move or a last-ditch effort to roll positions.

Micron call buyers are hitting bids on a 15% OTM strike with only two days to expiration, with today's volume already double the existing open interest and an additional 6k contracts traded this hour. The $4.1M premium and 100% implied volatility suggest a highly speculative bet on a massive, immediate move higher, but the bid tape indicates sellers are readily exiting positions rather than aggressively exiting.

Micron call buyers are aggressively lifting offers on a short-dated strike, with today's volume already exceeding open interest by nearly 3x and an additional 13k contracts trading this hour. The 5.6% out-of-the-money strike with just one day to expiration, coupled with a substantial $22M premium and 105% implied volatility, signals a highly tactical bet on an immediate, significant price-outsize move.

Micron call buyers are showing conviction with volume at 2.5x open interest, and an extra 8k contracts printed this hour alone. The 22% out-of-the-money strike with only one day to expiration and 125% implied volatility suggests a highly speculative, short-term bet on a massive intraday move, despite the mid-market tape showing no particular urgency.

Micron call buyers are aggressively lifting offers above the mid-market for 8-day 11% OTM contracts, with today's volume already exceeding open interest and an additional 8k contracts printed this hour. The substantial $24.8M premium and 108% implied volatility suggest a strong conviction in an outsized move higher before expiration, rather than a passive hedge.

Micron call buyers are aggressively lifting offers on a 33% out-of-the-money strike with only one day to expiration, with today's volume already double the existing open interest and 7000 contracts added this hour. The $0.5M premium and 140% IV suggest a highly speculative bet on an outsized intraday move, not patient positioning.

Micron put buyers are aggressively taking size above the ask, with today's volume already more than double the open interest and an additional 5.5k contracts traded this hour. The $10.1M premium on a 5.6% OTM strike with only one day to expiration is a strong signal of a tactical bet on a significant downside move, especially given the elevated 117% implied volatility.

Micron call buyers are aggressively lifting offers 16% out of the money with only one day to expiration, with today's volume already exceeding open interest and an additional 4200 contracts traded this hour alone. The $1.6M premium and 118% implied volatility suggest a highly speculative bet on a massive intraday move, not patient positioning.

The 8-day PLTR $126 put saw volume 7x its open interest this hour, indicating new positions are being established, but the mid-market tape offers no urgency. The 6% out-of-the-money strike and 45% implied volatility suggest a tactical bet on a near-term downside move, though the $0.7M premium is not a massive conviction check.

This hour's 5000+ INTC put contracts traded, more than triple the existing open interest, suggests new positions are being opened rather than closed. The 15 days to expiration and 24% out-of-the-money strike indicate a tactical bet on a significant downside move, especially given the high 93% implied volatility which prices in large price swings.

AVGO call buyers are aggressively lifting offers on expiring contracts, with today's volume already 16x open interest and an additional 5k contracts added this hour. The 7.7% out-of-the-money strike with zero days to expiration and 53% implied volatility suggests a highly speculative bet on a large, immediate price move.

The QQQ $685 put, expiring today and 6% out of the money, saw significant volume this hour with 4.8k contracts trading on top of existing open interest, indicating new bearish positions are being established. Buyers are aggressively lifting offers on this short-dated, 40% IV contract, but the minimal premium suggests a tactical, perhaps hedging, move rather than a large conviction bet on a massive move.

Buyers are aggressively lifting offers on the HD $335 call, with today's volume already 8x open interest and an additional 4k contracts traded this hour alone. This 5% OTM strike with only one day to expiration, despite the modest $0.2M premium, is a highly tactical bet on an outsized intraday move.

The 8-day ORCL $170 put saw 3x its open interest trade this hour, with volume at 5k contracts and 4k of that printing in the last 60 minutes. This is a tactical bet on a sharp move lower, 10% out of the money, but the mid-market tape and 53% IV don't show much urgency or pricing for an immediate catalyst.
